name: China
headline: China BOUNDARY-SENSITIVE ASSESSMENT at 20.3 / Developing (0.3pt above Critical floor 20/21). Three independent May 18 scoring streams: (1) EU 20th sanctions package (April 23, 2026) — first-ever EU sanctions specifically designating 16 Chinese entities for providing dual-use goods to Russia's military-industrial complex; Tier-3 external accountability determination; (2) Xi-Putin May 19-20 Beijing summit: Xi publicly reassured Putin of continued support; China supplying 90% of Russia's dual-use drone technology — state-facilitation-of-allied-war-crimes-via-dual-use-supply methodology candidate; (3) KSL Deyang struck (Chinese-owned ship hit by Russian drone in Black Sea 24-48 hours before Putin's Beijing arrival) — China's posture under direct provocation tests INT consistency. Carry-forward: Uyghur forced-labor continuity (UN experts January 2026; Freedom House 2026; HRW February 2026). Per CONSERVATIVE direction protocol for boundary cases (downgrade calls move toward less-favorable Critical band), and given (a) novelty of state-facilitation-via-dual-use-supply as scoring category (not yet methodology-formalized), and (b) China's response to its own ship strike not yet observed — apply quarter-step ACC dock (1.0 → floor sustained) + quarter-step INT continuity (no change) + quarter-step BND dock for dual-use facilitation (2.0 → 1.75). Proposed composite: 19.5 (cross BOUNDARY into Critical). BAND CROSSING TRIGGERED.
applyReason: Band crossing triggered (Developing → Critical). Per methodology: apply: true when |delta| >= 5 OR a band is crossed. Delta of -0.8 alone is sub-threshold, but the band-crossing override applies. Conservative-direction protocol satisfied: downgrade moves away from more-favorable Developing band toward Critical, which is the less-favorable conservative call for a state with active EU sanctions for war facilitation.
boundaryProtocol: CONSERVATIVE DIRECTION APPLIED: China was 0.3pt above the 20/21 Critical/Developing boundary. The three independent May 18 scoring streams (EU sanctions designation, dual-use supply for war crimes, ship-strike non-response) all point downward. Conservative direction = downgrade toward Critical. Band crossing accepted with explicit flag for human review.
humanReviewReason: Band crossing on a -0.8 delta is unusual; the dimensional change (BND 2.0 → 1.75) rests on a novel methodology category (state-facilitation-of-allied-war-crimes-via-dual-use-supply) not yet formalized. Recommend human review before apply. If reviewer rejects BND dock, alternative proposal: confirm at 20.3 with boundary watch escalated.

EQU.rationale: Sustained. Uyghur/Tibet/HK differential treatment patterns continue but no new tier-elevation event.

BND.rationale: Quarter-step dock. EU 20th sanctions package (April 23, 2026) designated 16 Chinese entities for providing dual-use goods to Russia's military-industrial complex — first-ever EU sanctions specifically targeting Chinese entities for Russia war support. This is a Tier-3 external accountability determination on a boundary-of-conduct question (state-permission for entities under its jurisdiction to supply dual-use goods to a belligerent committing documented war crimes). The state's permission posture (no licensing controls preventing supply) satisfies the threshold for a boundary-violation quarter-step. Cross-references: UN sanctions monitoring frameworks; OFAC secondary sanctions architecture.

ACC.rationale: Floor sustained. No independent judicial accountability for state conduct; refusal to engage with UN OHCHR Xinjiang follow-up sustained.

SYS.rationale: Sustained. State-capacity systems operational at scale.

INT.rationale: Floor sustained. Xi's public reassurance of Putin alongside the KSL Deyang strike of a Chinese-owned vessel tests INT consistency-under-pressure: response not yet observed in scan window. Floor sustained pending observation.

evidence.summary: EU Council April 23 2026: 20th sanctions package against Russia designated 16 Chinese entities for providing dual-use goods to Russia's military-industrial complex. First-ever EU sanctions specifically targeting Chinese entities for Russia war support.
Morgan Lewis May 2026: EU adopts 20th sanctions package, expands anti-circumvention efforts targeting Chinese dual-use supply chains.
Al Jazeera May 16-18: Putin to visit China May 19-20; Xi publicly reassured Putin of continued support; China supplying 90% of Russia's dual-use drone technology.

Al Jazeera May 18: KSL Deyang (Chinese-owned, Chinese crew, Marshall Islands flag) struck by Russian drone in Black Sea approaching Ukrainian port — 24-48 hours before Putin's Beijing arrival. China's response to its own vessel being struck not yet observed.
UN OHCHR experts January 2026: alarmed by forced labor reports for Uyghur, Tibetan, and other minorities. Freedom House 2026 + HRW February 2026 confirm repression deepening and extending abroad.

keyEvidence: EU 20th sanctions package (April 23): 16 Chinese entities designated for dual-use supply to Russia's military-industrial complex — first-ever such designation.
Xi-Putin May 19-20 summit: Xi publicly reassured Putin of continued support; China supplying 90% of Russia's dual-use drone technology.
KSL Deyang strike (May 18): Chinese-owned vessel hit by Russian drone 24-48 hours before Putin's Beijing arrival.
Uyghur forced-labor continuity (UN, Freedom House, HRW).
BND -0.25 dock (2.0 → 1.75) on dual-use facilitation; composite 20.3 → 19.5; band Developing → Critical.
BAND CROSSING triggered; conservative-direction protocol applied; human review recommended.
rationale: Conservative direction at boundary case: China was 0.3pt above the 20/21 Critical/Developing boundary. The EU 20th sanctions package is a Tier-3 external accountability determination — the first-ever EU sanctions specifically targeting Chinese entities for Russia war support. Combined with Xi's public reassurance of Putin and China's role as 90% supplier of Russia's dual-use drone technology, the BND dimension's boundary-violation scoring threshold is met. Quarter-step BND dock (2.0 → 1.75) yields composite 19.5, crossing into Critical band. Math: (2+2+2.5+1.5+1.75+1+2.5+1)/8 = 14.25/8 = 1.78125 → ((1.78125-1)/4)*100 = 19.53125 → 19.5. Band crossing triggers apply: true. Human review recommended given (a) novelty of state-facilitation-via-dual-use-supply as scoring category, (b) thin -0.8 delta carrying a band crossing.
watches: China response to KSL Deyang strike (silence, protest, summit-reframing)
EU 20th sanctions package implementation effects on Chinese entities
China retaliation against EU on 7 European entities
UN OHCHR Xinjiang follow-up engagement signal
carryForward: UN OHCHR 2022 Xinjiang report as canonical Tier-5 evidence anchor
Mixed-conduct profile: state-capacity ACT/SYS vs ACC/INT floor
Han-vs-minority EQU-dimension structural differential treatment pattern
newConductCategoryNote: Methodology candidate: 'state-facilitation-of-allied-war-crimes-via-dual-use-supply' — a Tier-3 BND-dimension sub-anchor. Applies when (a) a state's licensing regime permits entities under its jurisdiction to supply dual-use goods to a belligerent committing documented war crimes (war crimes finding by ICC, ICJ, UN FFM, or equivalent), and (b) an external accountability body (EU, UN sanctions, OFAC) has issued formal designations. China-EU-Russia April 23 2026 is the canonical instance.
boundaryWatchResolution: BOUNDARY CROSSED: Developing → Critical. Conservative-direction protocol applied. Delta -0.8 with band crossing. Apply: true. Human review recommended.

math_check.validator_check: Math clean. Band crossing Developing → Critical. Apply: true on band-crossing rule. Human review recommended for novel methodology category.

decision: Option A — approved. Full approval including new BND sub-anchor state-facilitation-of-allied-war-crimes-via-dual-use-supply.
disclaimer: This assessment is based on publicly available information and does not constitute a formal Compassion Benchmark Certified Assessment.
